runs.raw:
Looking at the code, the docstring says "Evict least-recently-used keys until the cache is strictly under capacity" and "Eviction continues until `len(order) < capacity`". The condition `len(order) > capacity` means eviction stops when `len(order) <= capacity`, so the list can still have exactly `capacity` entries after eviction.

However, `_trim_oldest_entries` is called with `target_len = capacity`, and it removes entries while `len(order) > target_len`. So entries are removed until `len(order) <= capacity`.

The outer loop condition `len(order) > capacity` and the inner function both use `> capacity` (not `>= capacity`). This means the cache can hold exactly `capacity` entries, not strictly fewer than `capacity`.

The docstring says "strictly under capacity" and "len(order) < capacity", but the implementation allows `len(order) == capacity`. The faulty line is line 56 (outer while condition) and line 27 (inner while condition) - both should use `>= capacity` instead of `> capacity` to enforce strictly less than capacity.
